subject: TSL Daily — Mar 20, 2026
html:
TSL Daily — Friday, March 20, 2026

Meetings

No TSL meetings today. Most recent TSL-relevant meetings from this week:

Sam / DZ (Mar 19)

  • Home page wireframe finalized — three sections: Needs Attention, Pipeline Updates, Discovery. Pitch deck triage MVP nearly ready (drag-and-drop, collaborative feedback, decision tracking to build taste profiles).
  • Competitive analysis of Erasmus/Inference: small team (4), produces quantitative data room reports but misses negatives and struggles to build customer confidence — TSL’s qualitative + community angle is the key differentiator.
  • LPA/side letter analysis flagged as a strong product opportunity: identifying unique LPA terms and MFN clauses is high-value, especially for family offices.
  • DZ taking on fund data enrichment via batch AI prompts (5–10 funds at a time), using GP names and contact emails as seeds.

TSL/Allocator Ideas (+Charlotte) (Mar 16)

  • Charlotte walked through her full evaluation framework: 1–5 scoring, 4 P’s issue commentary, page-level citations for everything pulled from source docs — auditability is non-negotiable.
  • Confirmed interest in a formal advisory role but is holding off on LP introductions until the product meets her usage standard.
  • NY event locked for April 27, 6pm.
  • Sam committed to demoing pitch deck triage by ~Mar 23.

Open Tasks

No TASKS.md found — pulled from meeting notes and context.

Sam:

  • Ship pitch deck triage MVP and demo to Charlotte (~Mar 23 deadline)
  • Home page card layout updates (unlocks pitch deck triage integration)
  • Send Supabase field structure to David for fund database standardization

David:

  • Fund data enrichment — batch process 5–10 funds at a time with GP names, websites, LinkedIn
  • Prepare 5 pitch decks (mixed spinout/ex-founder) for Charlotte review
  • Formalize advisory agreement structure with Charlotte

Waiting on:

  • Charlotte: follow-up on advisory documentation requirements

Suggested Priorities for Monday

  1. [product] Ship pitch deck triage demo to Charlotte — this is the unlock for her formal advisory buy-in, LP introductions, and the product feedback loop that drives everything else right now.
  2. [gtm] David finalizes the 5 pitch decks for triage review and NY April 27 event prep advances concretely (venue, invite list, format).
  3. [product] LPA/side letter analysis scoping — Charlotte’s comment about unique LPA terms and MFN clauses is a genuine differentiation angle worth a brief spec before the NY event.

Sam / DZ (Mar 19, 2026) · TSL/Allocator Ideas +Charlotte (Mar 16, 2026) · Sam / David Weekly Catch Up (Mar 16, 2026)
